channel.title: #DigitalBlackOutIran
channel.description: Daily simulation of how much GDP the world and the top 10 economies would lose if their own internet were blacked out for as many days as Iran's.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 33
item.description: 2026-04-01 internet blackout simulation: day 33, world scenario $51.07B/day. United States leads at $14.93B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 32
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 32: world daily loss $50.61B, cumulative world loss $1.33T; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 31
item.description: 2026-03-30 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $50.15B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 30
item.description: Day 30 blackout simulation report: United States $14.52B/day, China $7.80B/day, world $49.68B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 29
item.description: SEO update for 2026-03-28: simulated blackout losses show United States at $14.38B/day and world exposure at $49.20B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 28
item.description: Internet blackout day 28: modeled daily loss reaches $48.72B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 27
item.description: 2026-03-26 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $48.23B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 26
item.description: Day 26 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $13.95B/day; world $47.73B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 25
item.description: 2026-03-24 internet blackout simulation: day 25, world scenario $47.23B/day. United States leads at $13.81B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 24
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 24: world daily loss $46.71B, cumulative world loss $934.29B; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 23
item.description: 2026-03-22 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $46.19B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 22
item.description: Day 22 blackout simulation report: United States $13.35B/day, China $7.17B/day, world $45.66B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 21
item.description: SEO update for 2026-03-20: simulated blackout losses show United States at $13.19B/day and world exposure at $45.12B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 20
item.description: Internet blackout day 20: modeled daily loss reaches $44.57B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 19
item.description: 2026-03-18 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $44.00B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 18
item.description: Day 18 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $12.69B/day; world $43.42B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 17
item.description: 2026-03-16 internet blackout simulation: day 17, world scenario $42.83B/day. United States leads at $12.52B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 16
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 16: world daily loss $42.22B, cumulative world loss $575.77B; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 15
item.description: 2026-03-14 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $41.58B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 14
item.description: Day 14 blackout simulation report: United States $11.96B/day, China $6.42B/day, world $40.93B/day.
